tax: The expected ladder is Domain > Kingdom > Phylum > Class > Order > Family > Genus > Species. Accepted inferred orphan species may skip intervening ranks when they are reconstructed stems without ordinary subordinate hierarchy.
ap: Rank mismatch warnings appear as a red bold exclamation mark and red underline on the entry name. These marks are editorial flags, not creature traits.
eco: Use this guide when moving branches, promoting or demoting containers, or deciding whether an entry is a true taxon, transformation pathway, divine status, or reference page.
beh: Convergent cave shapes, occupations, spell traditions, guild roles, and regional variants should not create ranks by themselves.
traitsText: A well-placed entry has a parent appropriate to its rank, siblings at comparable biological scope, and descendants that step downward through the ladder unless intentionally accepted as inferred orphan species.
abilities: Correct structural errors before prose polishing. Duplicate wrappers, one-child filler ranks, and role-based pseudo-species should be merged, moved, or converted into background details.
bg: This rule prevents ecological resemblance from being mistaken for descent in a catalogue full of cave convergence.
g: This guide defines rank placement discipline: an entry’s rank must describe what the entry is, not merely where it appears in the indentation of the tree.
